blocks.content: Thanksgiving. Kit's flight home would have been two hundred and forty dollars he didn't have. He had a short film due the Monday after break, half-finished. He told himself those were the reasons.
He told Daisy on a Sunday call, the week before.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm not going to make it home for Thanksgiving.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Oh.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The film's not where it needs to be. And the flight is —

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You don't have to explain it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You're going home though.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah. Driving down with Claire, she's dropping me in Tennessee.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Good. Tell your mom I said hey.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She'll make a comment about why you're not there.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Tell her the film.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She won't buy it.
A quiet beat.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'll be home for December. I promise.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: And Daisy. Call me on Thanksgiving. Doesn't matter what time.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I mean it. Call me.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Okay.
She pulled up to Millhaven on a Wednesday night. The streets looked the same. She hadn't realized how much she'd been expecting them to look different.

Her mom had the kitchen warm. Her old room had been waiting exactly as she left it. She sat on the edge of her bed with her coat still on and looked at her desk and the window and the ceiling and felt seventeen and nineteen at the same time, and thought about a porch, and picked up her phone.
i'm home
how does it feel
smaller. familiar. kind of breaks my heart a little
yeah. that sounds right
